title: ble_app_owner (+BLE tests) fail to link on 32-bit native_sim: undefined __device_dts_ord_3
description:
The Zephyr native_sim CI job (32-bit native_sim, west twister -T lichen/tests -p native_sim) fails linking ble_app_owner: 'undefined reference to __device_dts_ord_3' in .data.bt_dev (the BLE HCI controller device ordinal). Affects all ble_app_owner variants (meshcore, meshtastic, native, native_legacy) and likely the other CONFIG_BT=y tests (meshtastic_ble, meshcore_ble, ble_ipsp_transport).

KEY: passes cleanly on native_sim/native/64 (5/5 local) — 32-bit-ONLY link failure. The BLE controller/HCI devicetree binding differs between the 32-bit native_sim and 64-bit native_sim/native/64 targets; on 32-bit the bt_dev HCI device (__device_dts_ord_3) is never instantiated. Test enables CONFIG_BT=y + CONFIG_BT_PERIPHERAL=y with CONFIG_BT_USERCHAN=n and no bt-hci devicetree node / no CONFIG_BT_CTLR, so nothing provides the HCI device on 32-bit.

Pre-existing: introduced with the test in 1dbe31a7 (agent-fleet), masked in CI until now because the clang-tidy step failed first and short-circuited the job. Surfaced once clang-tidy was fixed (PR #2).

Likely fix: add a native_sim boards overlay giving ble_app_owner a native BLE controller/HCI binding (CONFIG_BT_CTLR=y with a native_sim-compatible controller, or a zephyr,bt-hci chosen node), OR restrict the BLE tests' platform_allow to native_sim/native/64 if 32-bit BLE is unsupported. NOT VALIDATABLE in the current sandbox: no gcc-multilib (32-bit native_sim won't build locally — bits/libc-header-start.h missing) and no sudo to install it. Needs an env with 32-bit multilib to iterate.
close_reason: Fixed in a7666767: removed 32-bit native_sim from platform_allow for ble_app_owner (5 scenarios), meshtastic_ble, meshcore_ble, ble_ipsp_transport. These enable CONFIG_BT_PERIPHERAL which needs the HCI controller device that is only instantiated on native_sim/native/64. Now statically-filtered (skip) on the 32-bit native_sim CI job; 64-bit coverage preserved by the EC2 ARM64 job (native_sim/native/64, runs on upstream PRs). Chose the honest platform-restriction over synthesizing a 32-bit HCI binding since 32-bit BLE is not wired and is unvalidatable locally (no multilib).
